title: Replace EdDSA verification with Poseidon-based commitment binding in AgentPolicy

artifacts.description: Simplified AgentPolicy circuit removing EdDSA verification; proves knowledge of credential preimage (secret, nullifier, scope, expiry) whose Poseidon hash matches the leaf committed in the operator-approved Merkle tree
Standalone template computing Poseidon(secret, nullifier, scope, expiry, operatorId) — the canonical credential commitment hash used as the Merkle leaf

On-chain enrollment contract that verifies the operator EdDSA signature at enrollment time, inserts the credential commitment into the Merkle tree, and emits an event — moving signature verification fully off-circuit

Formal writeup of the security model change: EdDSA moves from in-circuit to on-chain enrollment check; Merkle inclusion proves operator approval; threat model, trust assumptions, and attack surface analysis
Circomkit/snarkjs test suite: valid preimage witness, wrong preimage fails, Merkle path mismatch fails, nullifier collision resistance, scope bitmask enforcement
Shell benchmark script using snarkjs groth16 prove timing before/after; records constraint count via snarkjs info, proving time, and verification time
Constraint budget breakdown before (EdDSA ~30k) and after (~20k total), proving time comparison table, migration guide for SDK callers

steps: Audit existing AgentPolicy.circom: extract the EdDSA verification subgraph, count its constraints via snarkjs info, and document all signals that feed into it
Define the canonical credential commitment schema: Poseidon(secret, nullifier, scope, expiry, operatorId) and pin input signal ordering in CredentialCommitment.circom

Rewrite AgentPolicy.circom: replace EdDSA inputs (pubKey[2], signature[2], message) with raw preimage inputs (secret, nullifier, scope, expiry, operatorId); compute commitment hash internally and assert it equals the Merkle leaf
Verify Merkle inclusion path as before (MerkleProof template unchanged); nullifier and scope bitmask constraints remain intact

Update AgentRegistry.sol: add enrollCredential(commitment, operatorSig, operatorPubKey) entry point that calls EdDSA verify on-chain (ecrecover or BabyJubJub verifier) before inserting commitment into the tree
Regenerate trusted setup (Powers of Tau sufficient for ~20k constraints); recompile circuit and export new verification key and proving key

Update test vectors in agent_policy_commitment.test.ts: generate valid witnesses with new signal layout, assert proof acceptance; generate negative cases (wrong secret, tampered scope, bad Merkle path) and assert rejection
Run bench_agent_policy.sh before/after and record constraint count, prover wall-clock time, and peak memory; target <50% of original proving time
Update SDK witness builder (TypeScript) to drop EdDSA signing step and instead pass raw preimage fields; bump minor version
Write security-model-shift.md documenting trust boundary change, enrollment-time guarantees, and residual risks (operator key compromise window)
Write circuit-optimization-changelog.md with before/after constraint table and SDK migration guide; open PR with all artifacts
dependencies: snarkjs groth16 toolchain (compile, setup, prove, verify)
BabyJubJub or ECDSA on-chain verifier for AgentRegistry.sol enrollment
Existing credential Merkle tree root management in AgentRegistry.sol
